2026-07-21 08:31:52

Market Last Trading Day - U.S. equities finished mixed during the last trading day as investors weighed earnings results, economic data, and the outlook for interest rates. Following Friday's confirmed regime change, TurboRegime remained in its newly established RiskOff allocation throughout the session. Market action did not materially alter the model's outlook, and the defensive positioning remains appropriate based on current conditions.

Market Today - TurboRegime begins Tuesday in the RiskOff regime with no additional trades currently indicated. While investors continue to monitor corporate earnings, economic reports, and market sentiment, the strategy remains focused on objective end-of-day market data rather than attempting to anticipate short-term price movements. The current portfolio allocation remains 60% SPY, 40% SHV, and 0% SPXL until market conditions provide confirmation that increased equity exposure is warranted.

Current Risk Regime - RiskOff remains active following the confirmed regime transition on July 17. The current trade is in its first full trading day and is essentially unchanged, with a return of -0.1% and a maximum drawdown of -0.1%. Although recent market weakness prompted the move to a more defensive allocation, TurboRegime remains substantially invested in the market while reducing overall portfolio risk. The strategy will continue monitoring the TReg Regime Signal each day and will increase exposure only after market conditions objectively support doing so.

Hold or Trade - HOLD 

Live Performance - TurboRegime continues to significantly outperform a buy-and-hold investment in the S&P 500 since live tracking began on April 1. More importantly, live execution continues to closely mirror historical expectations, with cumulative live performance remaining within just 0.1 percentage points of the historical backtest over the identical period. Since May 18, however, market conditions have been characterized by frequent reversals and limited follow-through, creating a challenging environment for trend-following strategies. During this period, TurboRegime has adapted by adjusting portfolio exposure as conditions changed rather than remaining fully invested. While several recent RiskOn and RiskOff trades have produced only modest gains or losses, they have generally reduced downside exposure relative to a fully invested S&P 500 portfolio because both regimes maintain allocations to short-term Treasury securities. The objective is not to avoid every losing trade, but to consistently lose less than the market during periods of weakness while remaining positioned to participate when stronger market trends emerge.

General Commentary - One of the most common mistakes investors make is evaluating a strategy based on a single trade rather than the complete investment process. Markets naturally alternate between sustained trends and periods of indecision. During strong trends, TurboRegime seeks to maximize participation. During choppy, sideways markets, the emphasis shifts toward protecting capital and managing risk until higher-probability opportunities return. Long-term success is achieved not by winning every trade, but by consistently making disciplined, data-driven decisions that improve portfolio performance over complete market cycles.
